title: UAS Laboratory

preview.teaser: Comprehensive UAS laboratory equipment for educational institutions. Compliant with national project requirements.

subtitle: Comprehensive equipment for unmanned aerial systems laboratories
intro: Establishing a UAS laboratory at an educational institution opens opportunities for in-depth practical training of specialists in UAS development, production and operation, and significantly increases chances of successful participation in open selection of providers and educational programs.

goals.title: Key Goals of the UAS Laboratory

items.1: Developing human capital in the aviation industry
items.2: Expanding the range and accessibility of research in the priority area of 'Intelligent transport and telecommunications systems, including autonomous vehicles'
items.3: Creating promising results of scientific, technical and innovative activities in areas demanded by the UAS market
items.4: Improving the effectiveness of natural science and technology educational programs

regulation.title: Regulatory Framework
regulation.intro: Establishing and operating UAS laboratories in educational organizations contributes to:

items.1: Critical technology 'Transport technologies for various applications, including unmanned and autonomous systems' (Presidential Decree No. 529, June 18, 2024)
items.2: National goals 'Technological Leadership' and 'Realizing Every Person's Potential' (Presidential Decree No. 309, May 7, 2024)
items.3: NTI roadmaps 'Aeronet' and 'Circle Movement' (Government Decree No. 317, April 18, 2016)

equipment.title: Laboratory Composition
equipment.intro: The technical core of the laboratory is the patent-protected compact FlowTech wind tunnel.

items.3: Flow visualization module (smoke machine + laser knife)

credentials: Our competencies are confirmed by our NTI Fund portfolio company status and winning the Drone Garage technology solutions competition at Archipelago 2025.
